description:
You are acting as an MLOps engineer who is organising a small repository of
  experiment artefacts that currently lives under
  “/home/user/experiment_artifacts”.  
  Inside that directory there is a plain-text file called
  “artifact_index.txt”.  Each line of this file is **one** artefact file name;
  duplicates mean that the same artefact was produced multiple times by
  different experiment runs.

  Goal  
  ────  
  1. Calculate how many times each artefact name appears in
     “/home/user/experiment_artifacts/artifact_index.txt”.
  2. Write those counts to a new file named
     “/home/user/experiment_artifacts/frequency_summary.txt”.

  Exact output format that will be tested  
  ───────────────────────────────────────  
  • One artefact per line, followed by exactly one single space, followed by the
    integer count (no leading zeros), then a newline.  
  • The list **must be ordered**:
        a. Primary key: descending numerical frequency (highest count first).  
        b. Secondary key: for lines with the same frequency, alphabetical (the
           usual byte-wise sort order of the “sort” command without any
           locale-specific options).  
  • Do not add header, footer or blank lines.

  Constraints  
  ───────────  
  • Do **not** alter the original “artifact_index.txt”.  
  • Create the summary file alongside it, using the exact name and path given
    above.  
  • A one-liner based on the standard GNU coreutils utilities
    (e.g. “sort”, “uniq”, “awk”, etc.) is enough, but you may compose the
    command however you like.  Shell redirection is allowed.

  Success criteria  
  ───────────────  
  The automated grader will confirm that:

  1. “/home/user/experiment_artifacts/frequency_summary.txt” exists.
  2. It is a regular file (not a directory or symlink) and world-readable.
  3. Its **exact contents** (byte-for-byte) follow the format and ordering
     rules explained above.
truth:
<!--
    Initial fixture inside the container
  -->
  <initial_files>
    <file path="/home/user/experiment_artifacts/artifact_index.txt"><![CDATA[
model_v1.bin
metrics_v1.json
model_v2.bin
metrics_v2.json
model_v2.bin
logs_v2.txt
model_v1.bin
summary_v1.pdf
metrics_v1.json
logs_v2.txt
model_v3.bin
metrics_v3.json
model_v3.bin
model_v3.bin
]]></file>
  </initial_files>

  <!--
    Expected end-state created by the agent
  -->
  <expected_files>
    <file path="/home/user/experiment_artifacts/frequency_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
model_v3.bin 3
logs_v2.txt 2
metrics_v1.json 2
model_v1.bin 2
model_v2.bin 2
metrics_v2.json 1
metrics_v3.json 1
summary_v1.pdf 1
]]></file>
  </expected_files>

  <!--
    File-system permissions (octal, relative to user)
  -->
  <permissions>
    <file path="/home/user/experiment_artifacts" mode="755"/>
    <file path="/home/user/experiment_artifacts/artifact_index.txt" mode="644"/>
    <!-- The agent must create the summary file with 644 or more permissive. -->
  </permissions>
